compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
# S5 — Trusted-proxy passthrough verifier

## Why
Many organizations terminate authentication at an edge proxy / gateway (oauth2-proxy, API Gateway,
ALB) and want the backend to trust the already-authenticated identity — and it is the only in-scope
path for identity systems that are not token issuers (e.g. GitHub-user via oauth2-proxy). This
verifier lets rebar delegate auth to that fronting proxy safely, through the SAME composite contract
as token verifiers.

## What
Add a **trusted-proxy** mode to `src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py`, activated by including `proxy` in the `mcp.auth_strategies` list (the strategy-selection
config key **introduced by S2**, this story's dependency — not a pre-existing seam), with flat config keys it registers: `auth_proxy_secret_env` (NAME of an env
var holding the proxy shared secret — no plaintext secret in config), `auth_proxy_secret_header`
(default `x-proxy-auth`), `auth_proxy_identity_header` (default `x-forwarded-user`), and
`auth_proxy_scopes` (comma-separated fixed scope set granted to proxy-authenticated principals;
OPTIONAL, defaults to an EMPTY set — a proxy-authenticated principal then holds no scopes and is
denied 403 by any non-empty `auth_required_scopes` until scopes are configured). It
comprises a small ASGI middleware plus a `TokenVerifier`-shaped component so that proxy identity
flows through the **same composite**, not around it:
- The **middleware** runs before the MCP app: it validates the shared-secret header (constant-time)
  and, only if valid, extracts the identity header into a request-scoped principal; otherwise it
  strips it. It **strips the entire `X-Forwarded-*` family** (and the configured identity/secret
  headers) from any inbound request that does not present the valid secret, normalizing header-name
  case and underscore/dash variants to defeat smuggling.
- The **verifier** turns that verified request-scoped identity into an `AccessToken` with
  `client_id`=the identity, `scopes`=`auth_proxy_scopes`, and **`resource=auth_resource_server_url`**
  so it passes the composite audience check exactly like a token verifier. If no verified proxy
  identity is present it returns None (composite falls through / denies) — it never accepts on a
  missing/invalid secret.
- **Ordering:** the proxy verifier participates in the composite `auth_strategies` order like any
  other; a bare request with no valid proxy identity is not accepted by it, so a co-configured bearer
  verifier still gets its turn. Adds the `auth_proxy_*` fields to `McpConfig`/`_SECTIONS['mcp']` (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`); their derived `REBAR_MCP_AUTH_PROXY_*` env-var names go in `MCP_ENV_VARS` (`src/rebar/mcp_server.py`) + `server.json`; `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes (docs regenerated by S7).

## Approach & rationale
Implement the oauth2-proxy-blessed trusted-header pattern, and route the result through the composite
(not around it) so the single audience/fail-closed choke point (S2) still applies. Bind HTTP to
loopback (S1 default) so only the proxy can reach the server; require a proxy shared secret; strip
any inbound identity/forwarded headers unless the secret is valid. This is the documented mitigation
for the forwarded-header-spoofing class (oauth2-proxy CVE-2026-40575 / CVE-2025-64484). A tiny ASGI
middleware is the right seam because forwarded identity lives on headers the token-only
`verify_token` seam does not see.

## Scope
The trusted-proxy verifier + its header-guard middleware only, on the S2 seam. Subset of the epic.
Depends on S2. Independent of S3/S4/S6.

## Security
Boundary-crossing and itself a spoofing surface. Hard requirements:
- **Loopback bind** is the intended deployment (S1 default); a non-loopback bind is governed by S1's
  existing non-loopback gate (both allowlists + TLS-at-edge ack) — no new bind key.
- **Operator prerequisite (documented, loud):** the fronting proxy MUST overwrite/strip all
  client-supplied identity and forwarded headers before adding its own; rebar additionally strips the
  entire `X-Forwarded-*` family on its side unless the shared secret validates.
- The env var named by `auth_proxy_secret_env` MUST be present and non-empty at startup or the proxy
  verifier refuses to start (fail-closed).
- The shared secret proves "from the proxy," not identity authenticity — so identity is trusted ONLY
  when the secret validates AND the header survived rebar's strip-then-repopulate; a constant-time
  compare gates it; header-name normalization defeats underscore/case smuggling.
- The minted `AccessToken` carries `resource=auth_resource_server_url`, so it is still subject to the
  composite audience check; fail-closed (no valid secret → no identity → 401).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The `auth_proxy_*` config fields exist in `McpConfig`/`_SECTIONS['mcp']` (`_config_schema.py`);
      their derived `REBAR_MCP_AUTH_PROXY_*` env-var names are in `MCP_ENV_VARS` (`mcp_server.py`) +
      `server.json`; and `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` passes (observable oracle: manifest drift
      test green; both files contain the new env-var names). Docs regenerated by S7.
- [ ] Unit test (tier: unit — the ASGI middleware + verifier exercised directly) asserts: with a
      valid `auth_proxy_secret` header, the identity header yields an `AccessToken` whose
      `client_id`=identity, `scopes`=`auth_proxy_scopes`, and `resource==auth_resource_server_url`;
      without the secret, the identity header is stripped and the request is unauthenticated (401).
- [ ] Unit test asserts a client cannot spoof identity: sending `x-forwarded-user` (or the
      underscore/case variants `X_Forwarded_User`, `X-FORWARDED-USER`) directly WITHOUT the valid
      secret is stripped and never trusted; and that the entire `X-Forwarded-*` family is stripped in
      that case (negative controls for each variant).
- [ ] Unit test asserts the verifier refuses to start when the env var named by `auth_proxy_secret_env`
      is absent or empty (observable oracle: startup raises a clear message).
- [ ] Unit test asserts `auth_proxy_scopes` defaults to an empty set when unset, so a valid proxy
      principal holds no scopes and is denied 403 by a non-empty `auth_required_scopes` (observable
      oracle: 403 with empty proxy scopes vs 200 once the required scope is granted).
- [ ] Integration test (httpx `ASGITransport`) asserts an authenticated proxy request (valid secret +
      identity header) → 200, and that the proxy-minted token still passes the composite audience
      check; a bare request → 401.
- [ ] Integration test asserts composition: with `auth_strategies=proxy,static`, a request with no
      proxy identity but a valid static token still succeeds (proxy verifier returns None, static
      accepts) — observable oracle: 200.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

## Compatibility
Additive, opt-in via the `proxy` strategy. No change to other verifiers or the stdio path.

file_impact.reason: add derived env-var names to MCP_ENV_VARS + wire middleware

compiled_state.title: S5 — Trusted-proxy passthrough verifier
